---
title: "Celigo_Data_Import"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-19"
---

```{r load-libraries}
library(data.table)
library(tidyverse)
library(rstudioapi)
```

```{r Object-Level-Import}
# === This is the generic 1st step in the import pipeline ===

# Specify how many runs will be included
runs_included = 3

# Read multiple files and then name them for graph legend etc
read_multiple_files <- function(n_files = runs_included) {
  Temp_List <- list()
  
    for(i in 1:n_files) {
    
    # Log
    cat("Selecting file", i, "of", n_files, "\n")

mask_input <- showPrompt(title = "Organizing Data:", message = "Enter Mask Type (e.g. MPO, DAPI, etc.)")
run_input <- showPrompt(title = "Organizing Data:", message = "Enter Source (e.g. Run 3)")

cat("Select Condition map:")

condition_map <- fread(file.choose())
names(condition_map) 

cat("Select data:")

file_path <- file.choose()

# Read and immediately aggregate
Object_Temp <- fread(file_path, 
                      skip = 17, 
                      header = TRUE,
                      select = c("Well", "Row", "Column", "X Position (µm)", "Y Position (µm)", "Area (µm²)", "Form Factor", "Smoothness", "Aspect Ratio", "Bright Field: Mean Intensity", "Bright Field: Integrated Intensity", "DAPI: Mean Intensity", "DAPI: Integrated Intensity", "MPO: Mean Intensity", "MPO: Integrated Intensity", "CitH3: Mean Intensity", "CitH3: Integrated Intensity"))

# Add columns preserving the origin of the data
    Object_Temp$Mask <- mask_input
    Object_Temp$Source <- run_input
    
    Object_Temp[condition_map, `:=`(Condition = i.Condition), 
                  on = "Well"]

   Temp_List[[i]] <- Object_Temp
    }
  
  # Combine all datasets
  combined_data <- do.call(rbind, Temp_List)
  return(combined_data)
}

     # Create a datasheet out of combined runs
Object_Table <- read_multiple_files(runs_included)
  
Object_Table[, row_id := .I]

setnames(Object_Table, "Area (µm²)", "Area")

setnames(Object_Table, gsub("[ _]", ".", gsub(":", "", names(Object_Table))))
    
#Object_Table[, Condition := gsub("\\\\n", "\n", Condition)]
Object_Table[, Condition := gsub("\\\\n", " ", Condition)]
#Object_Table[, Condition := gsub("\\s*\\n\\s*", " ", Condition)]

```

```{r Add-New-Data-to-Object-Table}
# === Robust data import with add/replace functionality ===

cat("=== Starting Data Import ===\n")

# Get user inputs
mask_input <- showPrompt(title = "Organizing Data:", message = "Enter Mask Type (e.g. MPO, DAPI, etc.)")
run_input <- showPrompt(title = "Organizing Data:", message = "Enter Source (e.g. Run 9)")

cat("\nMask:", mask_input, "\n")
cat("Source:", run_input, "\n")

# Load condition map
cat("\n--- Select Condition Map CSV ---\n")
condition_map <- fread(file.choose())
cat("Condition map columns:", names(condition_map), "\n")
cat("Condition map rows:", nrow(condition_map), "\n")

# Validate condition map
if (!"Well" %in% names(condition_map) || !"Condition" %in% names(condition_map)) {
  stop("ERROR: Condition map must have 'Well' and 'Condition' columns")
}

cat("Unique conditions in map:", paste(unique(condition_map$Condition), collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Load data file
cat("\n--- Select Data CSV ---\n")
file_path <- file.choose()
cat("Reading:", basename(file_path), "\n")

# Read data
Object_Temp <- fread(file_path, 
                     skip = 17, 
                     header = TRUE,
                     select = c("Well", "Row", "Column", "Area (µm²)", "Form Factor", 
                               "Smoothness", "Aspect Ratio", "Bright Field: Mean Intensity", 
                               "Bright Field: Integrated Intensity", "DAPI: Mean Intensity", 
                               "DAPI: Integrated Intensity", "MPO: Mean Intensity", 
                               "MPO: Integrated Intensity", "CitH3: Mean Intensity",
                               "CitH3: Integrated Intensity"))

cat("Rows read:", nrow(Object_Temp), "\n")
cat("Columns read:", ncol(Object_Temp), "\n")

# Sanity check: do we have data?
if (nrow(Object_Temp) == 0) {
  stop("ERROR: No data rows found in file")
}

# Rename columns to match existing format
setnames(Object_Temp, "Area (µm²)", "Area")
setnames(Object_Temp, gsub("[ _]", ".", gsub(":", "", names(Object_Temp))))
cat("Cleaned column names:", paste(names(Object_Temp), collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Add metadata columns
Object_Temp$Mask <- mask_input
Object_Temp$Source <- run_input

# Add Condition from map
cat("\n--- Mapping Conditions to Wells ---\n")
Object_Temp[condition_map, Condition := i.Condition, on = "Well"]

# Check for unmapped wells
unmapped <- Object_Temp[is.na(Condition), unique(Well)]
if (length(unmapped) > 0) {
  warning("WARNING: Wells without conditions: ", paste(unmapped, collapse = ", "))
  cat("Removing", sum(is.na(Object_Temp$Condition)), "rows with unmapped wells\n")
  Object_Temp <- Object_Temp[!is.na(Condition)]
}

# Fix escaped newlines in Condition
Object_Temp[, Condition := gsub("\\\\n", "\n", Condition)]
cat("Conditions mapped:", paste(unique(Object_Temp$Condition), collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Final sanity check on new data
cat("\n--- New Data Summary ---\n")
cat("Rows in new data:", nrow(Object_Temp), "\n")
cat("Wells:", length(unique(Object_Temp$Well)), "\n")
cat("Conditions:", length(unique(Object_Temp$Condition)), "\n")

# === Handle existing Object_Table ===
cat("\n=== Checking for Existing Data ===\n")

if (exists("Object_Table")) {
  cat("Object_Table EXISTS\n")
  cat("Current rows:", nrow(Object_Table), "\n")
  cat("Current sources:", paste(unique(Object_Table$Source), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  
  # Check if this Source already exists
  if (run_input %in% unique(Object_Table$Source)) {
    cat("\n*** WARNING: Source '", run_input, "' already exists! ***\n", sep = "")
    cat("Existing rows for this source:", nrow(Object_Table[Source == run_input]), "\n")
    
    response <- showPrompt(
      title = "Source Already Exists",
      message = paste0("Source '", run_input, "' already exists in Object_Table.\n",
                      "REPLACE = Remove old data and add new\n",
                      "CANCEL = Stop import\n\n",
                      "What would you like to do?"),
      default = "CANCEL"
    )
    
    if (toupper(response) == "REPLACE") {
      cat("\n--- REPLACING existing data for Source:", run_input, "---\n")
      rows_before <- nrow(Object_Table)
      Object_Table <- Object_Table[Source != run_input]
      rows_removed <- rows_before - nrow(Object_Table)
      cat("Removed", rows_removed, "rows\n")
    } else {
      stop("Import CANCELLED by user")
    }
  } else {
    cat("Source '", run_input, "' is NEW - will append\n", sep = "")
  }
  
  # Show column differences
  cat("\n--- Column Compatibility Check ---\n")
  existing_cols <- names(Object_Table)
  new_cols <- names(Object_Temp)
  
  missing_in_new <- setdiff(existing_cols, new_cols)
  missing_in_existing <- setdiff(new_cols, existing_cols)
  
  if (length(missing_in_new) > 0) {
    cat("Columns in EXISTING but not in NEW (will be NA for new rows):\n")
    cat("  ", paste(missing_in_new, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  }
  if (length(missing_in_existing) > 0) {
    cat("Columns in NEW but not in EXISTING (will be NA for old rows):\n")
    cat("  ", paste(missing_in_existing, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  }
  if (length(missing_in_new) == 0 && length(missing_in_existing) == 0) {
    cat("All columns match!\n")
  }
  
  # Append new data with fill=TRUE to handle missing columns
  cat("\n--- Appending New Data ---\n")
  rows_before <- nrow(Object_Table)
  Object_Table <- rbindlist(list(Object_Table, Object_Temp), fill = TRUE, use.names = TRUE)
  rows_after <- nrow(Object_Table)
  cat("Rows added:", rows_after - rows_before, "\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Object_Table DOES NOT EXIST - creating new\n")
  Object_Table <- copy(Object_Temp)
}

# Convert to data.frame if it was previously a data.frame
if (!is.data.table(Object_Table)) {
  cat("\n--- Converting to data.frame to match previous format ---\n")
  Object_Table <- as.data.frame(Object_Table)
}

# Regenerate row_ids for entire table
cat("\n--- Regenerating row.id ---\n")
if (is.data.table(Object_Table)) {
  Object_Table[, row.id := .I]
} else {
  Object_Table$row.id <- seq_len(nrow(Object_Table))
}

Object_Table[, Condition := gsub("\n", " ", Condition)]

# Final summary
cat("\n=== IMPORT COMPLETE ===\n")
cat("Total rows:", nrow(Object_Table), "\n")
cat("Total sources:", length(unique(Object_Table$Source)), "\n")
cat("Sources:", paste(unique(Object_Table$Source), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Total conditions:", length(unique(Object_Table$Condition)), "\n")

# Show breakdown by source
cat("\n--- Rows by Source ---\n")
if (is.data.table(Object_Table)) {
  print(Object_Table[, .N, by = Source])
} else {
  print(table(Object_Table$Source))
}

cat("\n--- Rows by Condition ---\n")
if (is.data.table(Object_Table)) {
  print(Object_Table[, .N, by = Condition])
} else {
  print(table(Object_Table$Condition))
}

# Cleanup temp object
rm(Object_Temp)
cat("\nTemp object cleaned up\n")
cat("Ready for analysis!\n")
```

```{r Well-Level-Import}
# === DATA IMPORT ===
runs_included = 1

read_multiple_files <- function(n_files = runs_included) {
  data_list <- list()

  for(i in 1:n_files) {
    cat("Selecting file", i, "of", n_files, "\n")

    user_input <- showPrompt(title = "Organizing Data:", message = "Enter dataset name. Next select the corresponding tabular .csv and condition .csv")
    
    file_path <- file.choose()
    data <- read.csv(file_path, skip = 15, header = TRUE, check.names = TRUE) %>%
      dplyr::select(1:39) %>%
      filter((Row %in% LETTERS[1:8]) & (Column %in% 1:12))
    
    condition_path <- file.choose()
    Condition <- read.csv(condition_path, check.names = FALSE)
    data <- data %>%
      left_join(Condition %>% select(Well, Condition), by = "Well")
    
    data$Source <- user_input
    data_list[[i]] <- data
    
    cat("Combining", file_path, "and", condition_path, "\n")
    cat("Added dataset:", user_input, "\n\n")
  }

  combined_data <- do.call(rbind, data_list)
  return(combined_data)
}

NET_Data_Combined <- read_multiple_files(runs_included)

NET_Data_Combined <- mutate(NET_Data_Combined, across(starts_with("X.."), ~as.numeric(gsub("%", "", .x)) / 100))
NET_Data_Combined$Condition <- gsub("\\\\n", "\n", NET_Data_Combined$Condition)
names(NET_Data_Combined) <- gsub("^X\\.\\.", "%", names(NET_Data_Combined))

NET_Data_Combined$MPO.Index <- as.numeric(NET_Data_Combined$'%MPO') * as.numeric(NET_Data_Combined$'AVG.MPO.Mean.Intensity')
NET_Data_Combined$CitH3.Index <- as.numeric(NET_Data_Combined$'%CitH3') * as.numeric(NET_Data_Combined$'AVG.CitH3.Mean.Intensity')
NET_Data_Combined$DAPI.Index <- as.numeric(NET_Data_Combined$'%DAPI') * as.numeric(NET_Data_Combined$'AVG.DAPI.Mean.Intensity')

setDT(NET_Data_Combined)
```

```{r Object-Lvl-Avg-and-Attach-from-CSV}
## ==============================
## == Import just an average ====
## == value from object level ===
## == data and attach it to =====
## == existing well level data ==
## ==============================

# Prompt for Source
source_input <- showPrompt(title = "Data Source", message = "Enter Source (must match existing data)")

file_path <- file.choose()

# Read and aggregate
well_averages <- fread(file_path, 
                      skip = 17, 
                      header = TRUE,
                      select = c("Well", "Area")) %>%
  group_by(Well) %>%
  summarize(
    Avg_Area = mean(Area, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_objects = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Source = source_input)  # Add Source to the averages

# Check if Source exists in NET_Data_Combined
if (!source_input %in% NET_Data_Combined$Source) {
  stop("Source '", source_input, "' not found in NET_Data_Combined. Available sources: ", 
       paste(unique(NET_Data_Combined$Source), collapse = ", "))
}

cat("Wells processed:", nrow(well_averages), "\n")
cat("Total objects:", sum(well_averages$n_objects), "\n")

# Join by BOTH Well AND Source
NET_Data_Combined <- NET_Data_Combined %>%
  left_join(well_averages %>% select(Well, Source, Avg_Area), by = c("Well", "Source"))
```

```{r Object-Level-Avg-and-Attach-from-DataTable}
# This averages object level data to use in the well level boxplot or platemap
well_averages <- Object_Table[, .(
 n_objects = .N,
  AVG.CitH3.Mean.Intensity = mean(CitH3.Mean.Intensity, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = .(Well, Source)]

NET_Data_Combined[well_averages, `:=`(
  Masked.AVG.CitH3.Mean.Intensity = i.AVG.CitH3.Mean.Intensity
), on = c("Well", "Source")]
```
